g.text: Token Type Enumeration & Data Structure Layout (scanner.py)
KEYWORD = auto() # if, else, while, return, true, false, null
EOF = auto() # end of input — parser terminator
lexeme | str | raw source text — NOT interpreted value
column | int | source column (1-based, first char of token)
KEY DESIGN: lexeme of NUMBER('42') is the string '42'
value conversion (int/float) is a LATER stage's job
KEY DESIGN: ERROR tokens carry position of bad char
scanning continues — all errors collected, not halted
2 | IDENTIFIER | 'x' | 1 | 5 | not in keyword table → IDENTIFIER
type | NUMBER | category: this is a numeric literal
lexeme | '42' | RAW source text — two chars '4','2'
column | 10 | position of '4', NOT of '2' (first char)
int('42') happens LATER in the eval/parse stage
float('3.14') — same rule — scanner stores raw lexeme only
type | KEYWORD | found in KEYWORDS dict after full word scan
WHY NOT prefix-match? 'iffy' → scan ALL chars → 'iffy'
line | 1 | position after last char consumed
Parser calls advance() until it sees EOF — then stops.
Without EOF: parser hangs or reads past end of list.
Same pattern as: TCP FIN, Unix pipe close, HTTP Content-Length
4 | IDENTIFIER | 'z' | 1 | 5 | valid token after two errors
ALL errors collected — not stop-at-first. O(1) recovery cost.
line starts at 1 | 1-based, per POSIX convention
carriage return (0x0D)| column += 1, NOT line (avoids CRLF double-count)
snapshot at _begin_token() | token.line/col = first char, not last
ALL updates inside advance() ONLY — single source of truth
peek() never changes line/col — inspect only, no side effects

div.p: Scanner reads L→R, one char at a time. advance() consumes + updates line/column. peek() inspects without consuming.
